name: ISLA
summary: Tool for executing instructions specified in the Sail ISA description language symbolically, used heavily in CHERI verification. The IR here is the Sail specification of an ISA — a formal description of every instruction's semantics, expressed in Sail's typed functional language.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: ISLA is a tool for executing instructions specified in the Sail ISA description language symbolically, used heavily in CHERI verification work. The IR here is the *Sail specification* of an ISA — a formal description of every instruction's semantics, expressed in Sail's typed functional language.

sections.title: ISA spec as representation
sections.content:
Sail/ISLA's contribution to representations: an ISA can be a representation, queryable by symbolic-execution tools, formally verifiable, and the source of multiple downstream artifacts (emulators, decoders, formal models). ARM, RISC-V, MIPS, and CHERI all have Sail specifications; ISLA executes them for verification and bug-finding.

Memory-safety hardware concerns and capability semantics live in `data/cheri` (CHERI canonical); this record covers the Sail-as-IR angle.
